cerebrate.def: 思考、使用大腦、思維的人的人、思考地 (思)
cerebrate.ex: Researchers must cerebrate deeply to solve this complex puzzle.

cerebration.def: 思考、大腦活動、思維的人物 (單數)
cerebration.ex: Intense mental cerebration is required for this difficult task.

cerebrospinal.ex: The doctor tested the patient's clear cerebrospinal fluid.

cerebrum.ex: The human cerebrum is divided into two main hemispheres.

ceremonial.def: 儀式的、正式的、禮儀、禮節的人物 (單數)
ceremonial.ex: The guards were wearing their traditional ceremonial dress.

ceremonially.ex: The foreign leader was welcomed ceremonially at the palace.

ceremonious.def: 講究禮儀的、正式的人的人物
ceremonious.ex: Entering the room, he gave a very low and ceremonious bow.

ceremoniously.ex: The royal guests entered the grand ballroom ceremoniously.

ceremony.def: 典禮、儀式、禮節、客套的人物 (單數)
ceremony.col: wedding ceremony, opening ceremony, master of ceremonies (司儀)
ceremony.ex: A solemn opening ceremony was held in the local stadium.

cert.def: (口)必然的事、確定無疑的人人物 (單數)
cert.ex: His victory in the local race was considered a dead cert.

certain.def: 確定的、某個、可靠的、必然的人物、確定地 (定)
certain.col: for certain, a certain degree, make certain, to a certain extent
certain.ex: I am absolutely certain that I locked the back door today.

certainly.ex: I will certainly come to your graduation party next week.

certainty.def: 確定性、必然的事、確實的人物 (單數)
certainty.ex: He said with absolute certainty that the plan would work.

certifiable.def: 可證明的、可發證書的、(口)瘋的人人物
certifiable.ex: His latest crazy plan was considered to be truly certifiable.

certificate.def: 證書、執照、證明書、發給證書的人的人物、證明地 (證)
certificate.col: birth certificate, marriage certificate, merit certificate
certificate.ex: You need to show your birth certificate to get a passport.

certificated.def: 經證實的、有執照的人的人 (或物) (證)
certificated.ex: He is a highly experienced and fully certificated pilot.

certification.def: 證明、檢定、保證的人的人物 (單數)
certification.ex: Professional certification is required for this senior position.

certify.def: 證明、保證、證實的人的人、證實地 (證)
certify.ex: I can certify the absolute accuracy of these financial reports.

certitude.def: 確實、確信、必然性人物 (單數)
certitude.ex: She spoke with local moral certitude about her decision.

cerulean.def: 蔚藍色的、天藍色的人的人物 (單數)
cerulean.ex: The bright cerulean sky was completely cloudless and clear.

cervical.def: 頸部的、子宮頸的人的人物
cervical.ex: Regular screening can help to prevent deadly cervical cancer.

cervix.def: 頸部、子宮頸的人的人物 (單數)
cervix.ex: The doctor examined the patient's healthy cervix carefully.

cesarean/caesarean.ex: The baby was delivered successfully via a cesarean section.

cessation.def: 停止、中止、中斷的人的人物 (單數)
cessation.col: cessation of fire (停火), cessation of hostilities
cessation.ex: Both sides finally agreed to a cessation of all hostilities.

cession.def: 割讓、轉讓、讓與的人的人物 (單數)
cession.ex: The cession of territory followed several years of war.

cesspool.def: 糞池、污水坑、墮落地點人物 (單數)
cesspool.ex: The investigation revealed a moral cesspool of corruption.

cetacean.def: 鯨目動物 (如鯨、豚)人物 (單數)
cetacean.ex: The whale is the largest living marine cetacean on earth.

chafe.def: 擦傷、磨損、(口)激怒、擦傷的人的人、磨損地 (磨)
chafe.ex: The tight shoes began to chafe her heels as she walked.

chaff.def: 穀殼、糟糠、戲弄、嘲弄、戲弄的人的人物、嘲弄地 (糠)
chaff.col: separate wheat from chaff (去偽存真)
chaff.ex: It's hard to separate the important facts from the chaff.

chaffed.def: 被戲弄的、被嘲笑的人的人 (或物) (笑)
chaffed.ex: He was chaffed mercilessly by his friends about his hat.

chaffinch.ex: A colorful singing chaffinch sat on the low garden wall.

chagrin.def: 懊惱、委屈、氣憤、使懊惱的人的人、懊惱地 (懊)
chagrin.ex: To her great chagrin, she realized she had lost the race.

chain.def: 鏈條、一連串、束縛、用鏈扣住、鏈住的人的人物、束縛地 (鏈)
chain.col: chain reaction, supply chain, food chain, chain store
chain.ex: The massive iron chain was used to secure the heavy gate.

chair.def: 椅子、主席及職位、教授席、主持、擔任主席的人物、主持地 (椅)
chair.col: comfortable chair, take the chair (主持), rocking chair
chair.ex: Please take a seat and wait in the comfortable blue chair.

chairman.ex: The board chairman gave a long speech about future plans.

chairmanship.def: 主席職位、主席任期 (單數)
chairmanship.ex: The union flourished under his successful chairmanship.

chairwoman.ex: The local chairwoman organized the annual charity event.

chaise.def: 馬車、長沙發的人人物 (單數)
chaise.ex: She sat comfortably on the leather chaise longue reading.

chalk.def: 粉筆、白堊、用粉筆寫、粉筆畫、人的人的人物、粉筆地 (粉)
chalk.col: piece of chalk, chalk up (積欠/獲得), chalky soil
chalk.ex: The teacher used a piece of white chalk to write on the board.

chalky.def: 粉筆狀的、蒼白的的人的人物
chalky.ex: The medicine had a strange, slightly bitter chalky taste.

challenge.def: 挑戰、質疑、要求、考驗、挑戰的人的人物、挑戰地 (挑)
challenge.col: accept the challenge, face a challenge, major challenge
challenge.ex: Climbing the mountain was a major professional challenge.

challenging.def: 有挑戰性的、考驗人的的人物
challenging.ex: Teaching a class of rowdy kids is a truly challenging job.

chamber.def: 房間、會場、議院、室、腔的人的人物、議院地 (房)
chamber.col: chamber music, chamber of commerce (商會), council chamber
chamber.ex: The royal guests were led to their private sleeping chambers.
